steps.title: Calculate area of square S
steps.content: Square S has a side length of 4 cm. Using the formula for the area of a square, Area = (side)², we get: $$A_{\text{square}} = 4^2 = 16\text{ cm}^2$$

steps.title: Add both areas for total
steps.content: Triangle R has area 80 cm² and square S has area 16 cm². The total area is: $$A_{\text{total}} = 80 + 16 = 96\text{ cm}^2$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
